channel.title: Major Digest - Reliable and Comprehensive News Sources: politics
channel.description: Get the latest news online at Major Digest, the top online news resource for the most reliable news sources. Stay informed about the world around you!

image.title: Major Digest - Reliable and Comprehensive News Sources: politics

item.title: Texas reports new measles outbreak in West Texas
item.description: The outbreak in Gaines County, with a population of about 22,000, has grown since two cases were reported in January. All those who contracted measles were unvaccinated, health officials said.
item.dc:creator: Texas Tribune, By Pavan Acharya, KPRC 2

item.title: Trudeau says Trump is serious about Canada becoming 51st state: reports
item.description: Canadian Prime Minister Trudeau has reportedly said that President Donald Trump is serious about Canada becoming the United States’ 51st state.
item.dc:creator: Fox News

item.title: A federal judge won't immediately block DOGE access to the US Labor Department
item.description: WASHINGTON (AP) — A federal judge said Friday he would not immediately block Elon Musk's team from accessing systems at the Labor Department, which has investigated companies owned by the billionaire adviser to President Donald Trump. U.S.
item.dc:creator: LINDSAY WHITEHURST, Associated Press, KGET 17, NBC

item.title: Transgender people sue Trump administration over new passport policy eliminating 'X' gender marker
item.description: A group of transgender individuals is suing the Trump administration over a new policy that eliminates the "X" gender marker on passports.
item.dc:creator: Fox News

item.title: Newsom signs $50M 'Trump-proofing' legislative package
item.description: California Gov. Gavin Newsom called a special legislative session shortly after President Donald Trump's electoral victory to bolster the state's legal defense.
item.dc:creator: Fox News

item.title: Federal judge blocks Elon Musk's DOGE from accessing sensitive US Treasury Department material
item.description: A federal judge has blocked Elon Musk’s Department of Government Efficiency from accessing Treasury Department records that contain sensitive personal data such as Social Security and bank account numbers for millions of Americans.
item.dc:creator: Anthony Izaguirre, Associated Press, KPRC 2

item.title: Trump ending Kennedy Center board members' terms, naming himself chair in place of Rubenstein
item.description: President Trump on Friday said he would end the terms of multiple members of the Kennedy Center board and make himself chair of a new board, putting himself at the helm of the Washington, D.C., cultural institution.
item.dc:creator: Alex Gangitano, NewsNation

item.title: West Virginia leads 22 states suing over New York Climate Change Superfund Act
item.description: West Virginia led the charge against New York’s Climate Change Superfund Act, suing to block the law.
item.dc:creator: Johan Sheridan, Fox 5

item.title: Top federal agency with history of wasteful spending could be next DOGE target
item.description: Elon Musk's Department of Government Efficiency (DOGE) efforts appear ready to shift focus to the Social Security Administration, which is likely to spark criticisms from Democrats similar to those in response to DOGE's moves against the U.S. Agency for International Development (USAID).
item.dc:creator: Fox News

item.title: Trump tells Ishiba at the White House that he wants to slash the US trade deficit with Japan
item.description: WASHINGTON (AP) — President Donald Trump said Friday he wants to slash the U.S. trade deficit with Japan as he welcomed Japanese Prime Minister Shigeru Ishiba to the White House for their first face-to-face meeting.
item.dc:creator: AAMER MADHANI and MARI YAMAGUCHI, Associated Press, KGET 17, NBC

item.title: Judge temporarily blocks USAID purge, evacuations from host countries
item.description: A federal judge late Friday temporarily blocked the Trump administration’s plan to place thousands of U.S. Agency for International Development (USAID) employees on leave at midnight.
item.dc:creator: Ella Lee, NewsNation

item.title: Rape crisis centers see funding delays amid Trump administration spending upheaval
item.description: Groups addressing sexual violence report not getting expected payments from grants that they depend on to keep running.
item.dc:creator: Will Stone, MPR News

item.title: Trump's DOJ drops challenge to gender-affirming care bans but urges Supreme Court to issue decision
item.description: President Trump’s Justice Department on Friday abandoned the Biden administration’s Supreme Court challenge to gender-affirming care bans for minors, but the new administration urged the justices to still resolve the issue this term.
item.dc:creator: Zach Schonfeld, KGET 17, NBC

item.title: Are Americans paying attention to Musk and Trump's DOGE? Here's what people in Iowa had to say
item.description: URBANDALE, Iowa (AP) — Iowa Sen. Joni Ernst has hailed Elon Musk and the Trump administration's Department of Government Efficiency as a necessary force in Washington, D.C., calling it “a storm that is headed this way that will sweep over this city and forever alter the way it operates.
item.dc:creator: THOMAS BEAUMONT, Associated Press, KGET 17, NBC

item.title: How AI could replace government workers to reduce spending
item.description: Elon Musk and DOGE are reportedly using artificial intelligence to analyze federal spending and where cuts an be made
item.dc:creator: Robert Sherman, NewsNation

item.title: Arab, Muslim Americans blast Trump's 'grotesque' Gaza proposal
item.description: Arab American and Muslim leaders are blasting President Trump’s proposal to take over Gaza, warning that it’s antithetical to their beliefs and amounts to ethnic cleansing.
item.dc:creator: Jared Gans, NewsNation

item.title: GOP-led House panel to hold hearing on USAID
item.description: The GOP-led House Foreign Affairs Committee will hold a hearing on the U.S. Agency for International Development (USAID) on Feb. 13, as the Trump administration moved to effectively shut down the agency and institute a mass foreign aid freeze. Committee Chair Brian Mast (R-Fla.
item.dc:creator: Laura Kelly, NewsNation

item.title: Historic Black church given ‘Proud Boys’ trademark calls for stand against hate
item.description: A judge ordered the naming rights of the extremist group the Proud Boys be given to the Metropolitan African Methodist Episcopal Church in Washington, D.C.
item.dc:creator: Chandelis Duster, MPR News

item.title: NASA instructs employees to remove pronouns from all work communications
item.description: An email seen by NPR says the move is to comply with a presidential order to “restore biological truth” to the government.
item.dc:creator: Geoff Brumfiel, MPR News

item.title: 12 states to sue over DOGE access to payment systems containing personal data
item.description: “As the richest man in the world, Elon Musk is not used to being told ‘no,’ but in our country, no one is above the law," a statement from the Democratic attorneys general reads.
item.dc:creator: The Associated Press, PIX 11

item.title: NYC Mayor's Race Poll: Cuomo the favorite, Adams looking stronger
item.description: NEW YORK (PIX11) -- An exclusive poll from PIX11 News, Emerson College, and The Hill shows former Gov. Andrew Cuomo would hold an early advantage if he hops in the New York City Democratic mayoral primary — but Mayor Eric Adams still has a chance.
item.dc:creator: Henry Rosoff, PIX 11

item.title: Politics Friday: Blur of action in Washington, back to action in St. Paul
item.description: MPR News host Brian Bakst talks about the blistering change in Washington with U.S. Sen. Tina Smith. And the scuffle in the Minnesota House is over. We discuss how lawmakers struck a deal to gavel in the session.
item.dc:creator: Brian Bakst and Matthew Alvarez, MPR News

item.title: NJ lawmakers create new bill to combat impersonating first responders
item.description: WEST WINDSOR, N.J. (PIX11) - First responders can be some of the most trustworthy figures in our communities, but some will try to impersonate them to commit other crimes.
item.dc:creator: Jim Vasil, PIX 11

item.title: DeSantis, Florida GOP tensions spill out into open
item.description: Tensions are spilling out into the open between Florida Gov. Ron DeSantis (R) and members of his own party in the Sunshine State, pointing to a new discordant era of GOP-led politics in a state that has trended red over the last few cycles.
item.dc:creator: Julia Manchester, WFLA, NBC
